prompt:
Make the valley CLI available in the owner's development profile. Two changes:
1. Bump the the-valley flake input: nix flake lock --update-input the-valley. Verify the locked rev is current main of gunk-dev/the-valley AND that it exports packages.x86_64-linux.valley (the CLI landed on main today as bin/valley + a flake package wrapping it — verify with nix eval or by inspecting the locked rev's flake.nix; if the package is absent, STOP and report rather than guessing).
2. Add the package to the owner's development profile: find where cosmo declares Patrick's dev tooling (home/dev.nix or equivalent home-manager profile — grep for where similar CLI tools are added) and add inputs.the-valley.packages.${pkgs.system}.valley (adapt to however the file accesses inputs/system; match local idiom exactly). One short comment: the valley CLI (S1 integrator verbs: pending/review); ships from the engine repo.
Scope: exactly these two changes. Verify: nix flake check; nix eval .#nixosConfigurations.classic-laddie.config.system.build.toplevel.drvPath; and eval whatever home configuration includes the profile if separately evaluated (CI builds build-home targets — make sure those eval too: nix eval the relevant homeConfigurations drvPaths if present). PR body: two sentences — what it adds, and that the converge loop deploys it on merge.
